description: UH Parking & Transportation Services: Houston METRO public transit, student Q-Card 50% discount, COAST program
segments:
Parking and Transportation Services offers
eligible
faculty, staff and students the opportunity to join COAST (Coogs on Alternative and
Sustainable Transportation), and sign up for the METRO fare card incentive.

Participants receive
a maximum of $27.50 each month toward their METRO fare.

Routes, schedules and an
interactive trip planner
are available at
ridemetro.org
.

Lean how to ride METRO. METRO's Purple Line
provides frequent service between the University of Houston and downtown Houston.
Riders can use their Q-Card and pay the same fare as local bus service with free transfers.
How to Get a Student COAST METRO Q Card Log in to your myParking account in AccessUH.

Click the "Student METRO Card" icon Download and fill out the application form, making sure to read and check the agreement
boxes, then signing your name and filling out the date. Save the completed application.
Click the "Order Permits" icon. Follow the prompts and select "COAST Permits"
Follow the prompts and upload your completed COAST application. Once the application has uploaded, follow the prompts to submit it.

You will receive an email from Parking & Transportation once your application is reviewed
notifying you if you meet the criteria and providing you with information on picking
up your card from the Parking and Transportation Services customer service office.
If you do not meet the minimum eligibility criteria, the email will let you know.

In an effort to better serve UH’s ever-growing campus and community, Parking and Transportation
Services offers eligible faculty, staff and students the opportunity to join COAST
(Coogs on Alternative and Sustainable Transportation), an incentive program for individuals
who carpool or utilize alternative and sustainable options to get to campus.

Individuals who are interested in participating in COAST or learning more about it
can reach out to the COAST team by sending an email to
coast@uh.edu
.

Please note, COAST participants will be asked to sign a commuter pledge that states
that members of COAST agree to not participate in any other UH PTS subsidized programs.
Find out if you qualify to participate! COAST applications for the Fall 2026 semester opens August 1, 2026.
To apply for the COAST program as a student, please follow these steps:

Log in
to the Parking Portal via
AccessUH. Click the
"Student Metro Card"
icon. Download and complete the application form. Save the completed form to your
desktop.

Click the
"Order Permits"
icon. Select the
"Student COAST Permit"
from the list of options.

Continue through the prompts until you reach the upload screen. Upload your completed
COAST Application PDF
.

You will be prompted to
pay the $10 application fee. You will receive an email from
Parking & Transportation
once your application is reviewed:
If approved, you'll be given a time window to pick up your COAST card.
If denied, you'll be notified and may reapply if you meet the minimum eligibility
of
9 on-campus, face-to-face credit hours
.
If approved, you'll be given a time window to pick up your COAST card.

If denied, you'll be notified and may reapply if you meet the minimum eligibility
of
9 on-campus, face-to-face credit hours
.

* Since the COAST program does not run during the summer, all student cards will be
deactivated at the conclusion of the spring semester.

Faculty & Staff Application Process To apply for the COAST Metro program as a faculty or staff member, please follow these
steps:

Log in
to your Parking Portal. Click on the
"FS COAST Metro"
icon to open the application form.

Complete the form
in full. Navigate to the
"Order Permits"
section. Click on
"COAST Permits"
.

Follow the prompts to
upload your completed form
. You will be prompted to
pay the $10 application fee
.

Once your form and payment are submitted, you will receive a
confirmation email
with instructions for picking up your permit.

Lost COAST Card Process If a student loses their COAST card, there is a $25 replacement fee. To request a
new card, students must email
coast@uh.edu
.
